documents.ocrText: {"name":"AIML Hackathon Problem Statement-1 CHIPS (Chhattisgarh","raw_text":"AIML Hackathon Problem Statement-1 CHIPS (Chhattisgarh Infotech Promotion Society) PS 01 : AI Co-Pilot for CSC Operators Domain :AI for Citizen Services : GovTech Background A Common Service Centre operator in a district like Rajnandgaon may process 70 to 90 applications a day: pensions, certificates, licenses, corrections: on a slow portal with no real-time guidance and no tool to catch mistakes before they become rejections. She is the only human standing between the government system and the citizen. Every rejection bounces back to her desk. Problem Statement Design and build an intelligent assistant for frontline CSC operators that reduces form rejections and cuts per-application handling time without replacing the operator’s judg- ment. The system should understand the context of each service type, surface the right information at the right moment, and learn from the pattern of past errors to warn before a mistake is submitted. Your solution should work under real field constraints: intermittent connectivity, mixed literacy users on the citizen side, and operators who have no time to read a manual. Directions Worth Exploring •AI:Local NLP models that parse form fields and flag anomalies before submission even offline. •Data:Rejection pattern analysis: which form fields cause the most bouncebacks and for which service types. •Check:Pre submission eligibility inference from document metadata without stor- ing sensitive data. •Language:Operator facing voice or chat interface in Hindi or local language for contextual guidance mid form. 1 •Prediction:Application triage: predicting which submissions are likely to stall and surfacing them proactively. •Network:Graceful degradation: core intelligence available even on 2G or offline via edge cached models. Evaluation Criteria •Impact:Reduction in rejection rate and resubmission overhead. •Intelligence Depth:Quality of pre submission inference and error prediction. •Field Realism:Does it work under low bandwidth and time pressure. •UX for Operators:Zero learning curve: usable in under 60 seconds. 2"}

parsedFields.field_value: AIML Hackathon Problem Statement-1 CHIPS (Chhattisgarh Infotech Promotion Society) PS 01 : AI Co-Pilot for CSC Operators Domain :AI for Citizen Services : GovTech Background A Common Service Centre operator in a district like Rajnandgaon may process 70 to 90 applications a day: pensions, certificates, licenses, corrections: on a slow portal with no real-time guidance and no tool to catch mistakes before they become rejections. She is the only human standing between the government system and the citizen. Every rejection bounces back to her desk. Problem Statement Design and build an intelligent assistant for frontline CSC operators that reduces form rejections and cuts per-application handling time without replacing the operator’s judg- ment. The system should understand the context of each service type, surface the right information at the right moment, and learn from the pattern of past errors to warn before a mistake is submitted. Your solution should work under real field constraints: intermittent connectivity, mixed literacy users on the citizen side, and operators who have no time to read a manual. Directions Worth Exploring •AI:Local NLP models that parse form fields and flag anomalies before submission even offline. •Data:Rejection pattern analysis: which form fields cause the most bouncebacks and for which service types. •Check:Pre submission eligibility inference from document metadata without stor- ing sensitive data. •Language:Operator facing voice or chat interface in Hindi or local language for contextual guidance mid form. 1 •Prediction:Application triage: predicting which submissions are likely to stall and surfacing them proactively. •Network:Graceful degradation: core intelligence available even on 2G or offline via edge cached models. Evaluation Criteria •Impact:Reduction in rejection rate and resubmission overhead. •Intelligence Depth:Quality of pre submission inference and error prediction. •Field Realism:Does it work under low bandwidth and time pressure. •UX for Operators:Zero learning curve: usable in under 60 seconds. 2

She is the only human standing between the government system and the citizen. Every rejection bounces back to her desk. Problem Statement Design and build an intelligent assistant for frontline CSC operators that reduces form rejections and cuts per-application handling time without replacing the operator’s judg- ment. The system should understand the context of each service type, surface the right information at the right moment, and learn from the pattern of past errors to warn before a mistake is submitted. Your solution should work under real field constraints: intermittent connectivity, mixed literacy users on the citizen side, and operators who have no time to read a manual. Directions Worth Exploring •AI:Local NLP models that parse form fields and flag anomalies before submission even offline. •Data:Rejection pattern analysis: which form fields cause the most bouncebacks and for which service types. •Check:Pre submission eligibility inference from document metadata without stor- ing sensitive data. •Language:Operator facing voice or chat interface in Hindi or local language for contextual guidance mid form. 1 •Prediction:Application triage: predicting which submissions are likely to stall and surfacing them proactively. •Network:Graceful degradation: core intelligence available even on 2G or offline via edge cached models. Evaluation Criteria •Impact:Reduction in rejection rate and resubmission overhead. •Intelligence Depth:Quality of pre submission inference and error prediction. •Field Realism:Does it work under low bandwidth and time pressure. •UX for Operators:Zero learning curve: usable in under 60 seconds. 2

documents.ocrText: {"name":"AIML Hackathon Problem Statement-1 CHIPS (Chhattisgarh","raw_text":"AIML Hackathon Problem Statement-1 CHIPS (Chhattisgarh Infotech Promotion Society) PS 01 : AI Co-Pilot for CSC Operators Domain :AI for Citizen Services : GovTech Background A Common Service Centre operator in a district like Rajnandgaon may process 70 to 90 applications a day: pensions, certificates, licenses, corrections: on a slow portal with no real-time guidance and no tool to catch mistakes before they become rejections. She is the only human standing between the government system and the citizen. Every rejection bounces back to her desk. Problem Statement Design and build an intelligent assistant for frontline CSC operators that reduces form rejections and cuts per-application handling time without replacing the operator’s judg- ment. The system should understand the context of each service type, surface the right information at the right moment, and learn from the pattern of past errors to warn before a mistake is submitted. Your solution should work under real field constraints: intermittent connectivity, mixed literacy users on the citizen side, and operators who have no time to read a manual. Directions Worth Exploring •AI:Local NLP models that parse form fields and flag anomalies before submission even offline. •Data:Rejection pattern analysis: which form fields cause the most bouncebacks and for which service types. •Check:Pre submission eligibility inference from document metadata without stor- ing sensitive data. •Language:Operator facing voice or chat interface in Hindi or local language for contextual guidance mid form. 1 •Prediction:Application triage: predicting which submissions are likely to stall and surfacing them proactively. •Network:Graceful degradation: core intelligence available even on 2G or offline via edge cached models. Evaluation Criteria •Impact:Reduction in rejection rate and resubmission overhead. •Intelligence Depth:Quality of pre submission inference and error prediction. •Field Realism:Does it work under low bandwidth and time pressure. •UX for Operators:Zero learning curve: usable in under 60 seconds. 2"}

Problem Statement Design and build an intelligent assistant for frontline CSC operators that reduces form rejections and cuts per-application handling time without replacing the operator’s judg- ment. The system should understand the context of each service type, surface the right information at the right moment, and learn from the pattern of past errors to warn before a mistake is submitted. Your solution should work under real field constraints: intermittent connectivity, mixed literacy users on the citizen side, and operators who have no time to read a manual. Directions Worth Exploring •AI:Local NLP models that parse form fields and flag anomalies before submission even offline. •Data:Rejection pattern analysis: which form fields cause the most bouncebacks and for which service types. •Check:Pre submission eligibility inference from document metadata without stor- ing sensitive data. •Language:Operator facing voice or chat interface in Hindi or local language for contextual guidance mid form. 1 •Prediction:Application triage: predicting which submissions are likely to stall and surfacing them proactively. •Network:Graceful degradation: core intelligence available even on 2G or offline via edge cached models. Evaluation Criteria •Impact:Reduction in rejection rate and resubmission overhead. •Intelligence Depth:Quality of pre submission inference and error prediction. •Field Realism:Does it work under low bandwidth and time pressure. •UX for Operators:Zero learning curve: usable in under 60 seconds. 2
